query: Iran IAEA cooperation law suspension 2025 2026, current state of Iran nuclear inspections and diplomacy, US Iran nuclear deal negotiations status June July 2026
answer:
I've completed the research. Here's a summary of the key findings on Iran-IAEA cooperation, nuclear inspections, and US-Iran diplomacy as of July 4, 2026:

**IAEA Cooperation Suspension Law:** Iran's parliament passed the suspension law on June 25, 2025 (221–0 with 1 abstention), signed by President Pezeshkian on July 2, 2025. It remains legally in force. A conditional "Cairo Agreement" (Sept 2025) briefly allowed limited inspector return but Iran terminated it in November 2025.

**Current Inspections:** Per the June 4, 2026 IAEA reports, the agency **stopped all verification activities after Feb 28, 2026** — no routine inspections are currently happening. Iran denied access to 20 declared sites; last JCPOA-site inspections were Nov–Dec 2025.

**Nuclear Material (last verified June 13, 2025):** ~9,040 kg total enriched uranium, including **440.9 kg of 60%-enriched (near weapons-grade)**. ~22,000 centrifuges were destroyed in the June 2025 strikes. Loss of "continuity of knowledge" is a key IAEA concern.

**Diplomacy:** After the 2026 war (which killed Supreme Leader Khamenei), Trump and Pezeshkian signed the "Islamabad Memorandum" (June 17, 2026). The Lake Lucerne Summit (June 21–22) set a roadmap toward a final deal within ~60 days. Latest indirect talks occurred in Doha/Qatar on July 1–2, 2026 (Witkoff & Kushner vs. Gharibabadi), reporting "positive progress." A key unresolved dispute: the IAEA/US want prompt inspections under the MoU, while Iran insists inspections come only within a final deal conditioned on full sanctions relief.

The full synthesis and structured data (key facts, sources, data points) have been recorded via the StructuredOutput tool.

sources.snippet: Legislation aims to ensure support for Iran's rights, especially uranium enrichment; IAEA awaiting official information.

key_facts: Iran's parliament passed the 'Suspension of Cooperation with the IAEA' law on June 25, 2025 (221 votes for, 1 abstention); President Pezeshkian signed it into law July 2, 2025.
The IAEA stopped all verification activities in Iran after Feb 28, 2026, per the June 4, 2026 IAEA quarterly reports; no routine inspections are currently occurring.
IAEA has been denied access to 20 declared nuclear sites plus one location; last JCPOA-site inspections were Nov 3 and Dec 22-23, 2025.
Iran's last IAEA-verified 60%-enriched (near weapons-grade) uranium stockpile was 440.9 kg as of June 13, 2025; total enriched uranium ~9,040.5 kg (UF6).
About 22,000 gas centrifuges across Natanz and Fordow were destroyed/inoperable after June 2025 strikes.
A conditional 'Cairo Agreement' (Sept 9, 2025) briefly allowed limited inspector return but Iran terminated it Nov 20, 2025.
Trump and Pezeshkian remotely signed the 'Islamabad Memorandum' (MoU) on June 17, 2026 to end the 2026 war between Iran, the US, and Israel.
The June 21-22, 2026 Lake Lucerne Summit (US, Iran, Qatar, Pakistan) set a roadmap toward a final deal within ~60 days.
Grossi says the MoU 'explicitly' mandates IAEA-supervised inspections; Iran (Gharibabadi) insists inspections happen only within a final agreement conditioned on full sanctions termination.
July 1-2, 2026 indirect US-Iran talks in Qatar/Doha (Witkoff & Kushner for US; Gharibabadi for Iran) made 'positive progress'; next round after the supreme leader's July 4 funeral.
VP JD Vance said the Doha talks were 'going well' but flagged worry over the nuclear issue.
The IAEA suspension law has not been formally repealed; restoring inspections is being pursued through the diplomatic MoU track.

data_points.metric: Iran parliament vote to suspend IAEA cooperation (votes in favor)
Total enriched uranium stockpile (UF6, uranium mass), last verified
Gas centrifuges destroyed/inoperable after June 2025 strikes
Declared nuclear sites IAEA denied access to
Target timeframe for US-Iran final deal from Lake Lucerne Summit
